title: Birthday Girl Mirra Andreeva Fights Off Nerves to Reach Her First Madrid Final!
summary: Imagine it’s your birthday, and instead of cake, you’re playing the biggest tennis match of your life. That’s what happened to Mirra Andreeva! She turned 19 on Wednesday, and on Thursday, she battled her way into the Madrid Open final. She beat Hailey Baptiste 6-4, 7-6 (10-8) in a super exciting match. Andreeva was so close to winning in straight sets. She served for the match at 5-4 in the second set, but Baptiste broke her serve and forced a tiebreak (a special game to decide the set). In the tiebreak, Andreeva had to save set points—those are points that would have let Baptiste win the set. “I feel so much adrenaline inside,” Andreeva said. “I’m just so happy that I won and that I was able to save all those set points.” She’s now the second-youngest finalist in the tournament’s history, only behind Caroline Wozniacki. On Saturday, she’ll play Marta Kostyuk, who won her match earlier. But there’s a twist: Kostyuk refused to shake hands with her Russian-born opponent after winning. In the men’s tournament, a Belgian player named Alexander Blockx shocked everyone by beating the defending champion Casper Ruud. Blockx was unseeded (meaning he wasn’t expected to win) and had never won a clay-court match before this season! Now he’s in his first big semi-final.
why_it_matters: This story shows how even on a special day like your birthday, you can face a big challenge and succeed if you stay brave and focused.

keywords.explanation: A special game to decide a set when the score is tied at 6-6.
A rush of energy and excitement your body feels in important moments.
When a player is not expected to win a tournament.
The player who won the tournament last year.
Winning a match by winning two sets without losing one.
A player who reaches the last match of a tournament.

What was the final score of Andreeva's match against Baptiste?
What did Andreeva have to save during the tiebreak?
In the women's semi-final, what did Marta Kostyuk refuse to do?
Who did Alexander Blockx surprisingly beat?

background_read: The Madrid Open is a major tennis tournament played on clay courts in Spain each year.
Caroline Wozniacki, who held the record Andreeva nearly beat, is a former world No. 1 from Denmark who won a Grand Slam title.
Casper Ruud, the men's defending champion, is a top Norwegian player who has reached several Grand Slam finals.
Article_Structure: WHO: Mirra Andreeva, a 19-year-old tennis player celebrating her birthday.
WHAT: She reached her first Madrid Open final by beating Hailey Baptiste in a tense tiebreak.
WHERE: The Madrid Open, a big tennis tournament in Spain.
WHY: She had to overcome nerves and save multiple set points, showing her fighting spirit, while the men's side saw an unseeded player cause a huge upset.

perspectives.description: She was overjoyed to win on her birthday and couldn't believe she saved all those set points.
Hailey Baptiste fought hard to push the match to a tiebreak and almost won, so losing was tough.
Fans were shocked by the men's upset, where an unknown player beat the defending champion, making the tournament thrilling.
